entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The plant species Arachis hypogaea L. studied for its response to irrigation timing

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The application of water at different growth stages to the plants

entities.name: CONTROLLED ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The setting where the groundnut plants were grown

entities.name: SOIL MOISTURE DEFICIT
entities.description: The state of water shortage imposed between sowing and pod initiation or pod initiation and harvest

entities.name: POD INITIATION
entities.description: A specific growth stage of the groundnut plant

entities.name: FINAL HARVEST
entities.description: The end stage of the groundnut growth cycle

entities.name: 14C-PARTITIONING
entities.description: The study of the distribution of 14C-labeled carbon within the plant

entities.name: LEAVES
entities.description: Plant organs that serve as primary sites of 14CO2 fixation and sources of assimilates

entities.name: STEMS
entities.description: Plant organs that act as sinks and sites of 14CO2 fixation

entities.name: STEM APICES
entities.description: The growing tips of the stems that import assimilates and fix 14CO2

entities.name: PEGS
entities.description: Specialized structures in groundnuts that fix 14CO2 and maintain high turgor

entities.name: PODS
entities.description: The fruit-bearing structures of the groundnut that exhibit sink activity

entities.name: 14CO2 FIXATION
entities.description: The process of incorporating 14C-labeled carbon dioxide into plant tissues

entities.name: ASSIMILATES
entities.description: Organic compounds produced by photosynthesis and translocated within the plant

entities.name: TRANSLOCATION
entities.description: The movement of assimilates from source to sink tissues

entities.name: SINKS
entities.description: Plant parts that consume or store assimilates

entities.name: WATER STATUS
entities.description: The physiological state of water within the plant tissues

entities.name: TURGOR LEVELS
entities.description: The internal pressure of plant cells against the cell wall

entities.name: SOLUTE POTENTIALS
entities.description: The component of water potential resulting from dissolved solutes

entities.name: OSMOTIC ADJUSTMENT
entities.description: The accumulation of solutes to maintain turgor during water stress

entities.name: STRESS
entities.description: The physiological strain caused by soil moisture deficits

relationships.description: Response of groundnut to timing of irrigation
Grown in a controlled environment
Timing of irrigation determines the imposition of soil moisture deficits
Soil moisture deficits cause physiological stress
Method used to track and measure carbon fixation
Primary sites of 14CO2 fixation
Sites of 14CO2 fixation that increase upon stress release
Sites of 14CO2 fixation that increase upon stress release
Sites of 14CO2 fixation that increase upon stress release
Primary source of assimilates
Assimilates are moved via translocation
Initially the major sinks for assimilates
Exhibit sink activity for assimilates
Leaf water status shows diurnal variation
Maintain higher turgor levels than leaves
Lower solute potentials allow for higher turgor levels
Allows maintenance of higher turgor levels during severe stress
Occurs specifically in expanding leaves
Severe stress triggers osmotic adjustment
Release of stress increases fixation in stems, apices, and pegs
